mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM668 engine typically costs £1,200–£2,200 supply only, with used units available from £800–£1,500. Total installed cost is around £1,800–£2,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Vaneo.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the OM668 is generally a robust and reliable engine with a typical lifespan of 240,000–250,000 km. It's more reliable than the petrol M166. Common issues include injector problems, EGR valve failure, and piston ring wear.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injector and fuel system issues (rough running, stalling, hard starting), EGR valve failure (excessive smoke), piston ring wear (oil consumption), turbocharger issues, and a cramped engine bay that makes many repairs labour-intensive.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Vaneo values range from £1,000–£4,000 depending on condition. A £1,200–£2,200 reconditioned engine is 30–60% of the vehicle's value — apply the Mercedes 70% scrap threshold. If the car is a cherished example with good bodywork, replacement may be worthwhile.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is OM668.914 — it's the only 1.7-litre CDI used in the Vaneo. You can find it on the VIN plate, in the vehicle documentation, or on a label on the engine itself. Production ran from 2001 to 2005.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the OM668 injectors, or do they need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injectors can sometimes be cleaned or refurbished, but replacement is often the most reliable solution. Replacement costs £300–£800 per injector depending on the workshop. If you experience rough running or hard starting, have the fuel system diagnosed promptly. Catching injector issues early can prevent more serious engine damage.

hero.subHeadline: The Vaneo 1.7 CDI is powered by Mercedes' OM668 1.7-litre diesel — a compact, transverse four-cylinder that delivers impressive economy and a surprising turn of pace. It's a robust engine, but injector and EGR issues, plus a cramped engine bay, are well-documented. Get quotes from UK specialists for reconditioned, rebuilt and used units today.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Vaneo 1.7 CDI market. A reconditioned engine typically costs between £1,200–£2,200 supply only, with total installed costs ranging from £1,800–£2,800. Used units are available from £800–£1,500. See the Era Map above for generation-specific context.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The engine bay is notoriously cramped — many maintenance tasks require dropping the engine with the subframe.

cta.label: → Compare Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Injector and Fuel System Issues
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hard starting, black smoke, loss of power, stalling, engine warning light
problems.recommendation: Fuel injector issues are a well-documented problem on the OM668. The engine may cut out from a cold start and stall, or suffer from stalling during deceleration and RPM limiting above 3,500 rpm. The Bosch fuel system is generally reliable, but the fuel pressure regulator can fail. If you experience any running issues, have the fuel system diagnosed promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full injector failure guide

problems.title: EGR Valve and Turbocharger Problems
problems.symptoms: Excessive smoke, loss of power, rough idle, poor fuel economy, engine warning light
problems.recommendation: The OM668 can suffer from EGR valve problems, leading to excessive smoke and poor running. Turbocharger issues have also been reported. Regular maintenance and using quality fuel can help prevent these issues.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR and turbo failure guide

problems.title: Crankshaft and Piston Ring Wear
problems.symptoms: Excessive oil consumption, blue smoke, loss of power, knocking from the bottom end
problems.recommendation: Piston ring problems can occur on the OM668, leading to oil consumption. If you notice increasing oil consumption or blue smoke, have the engine inspected. While the OM668 is generally robust, these issues can develop at higher mileages.

failureLink.label: Read the full piston ring failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Vaneo 1.7 CDI — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Well-understood repair — catch before further damage
Labour-intensive but cheaper than a full engine
Replacement — catch before debris damages the engine
If the bottom end fails, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Vaneo 1.7 CDI? Check These First
buyIf: * Evidence of regular oil changes with correct MB-spec oil
* No excessive smoke or rough running
* Full service history — the OM668 is robust but needs care
* A well-maintained example — these are getting old now
avoidIf: * Excessive smoke from the exhaust — EGR or injector issues are likely
* Hard starting or stalling — fuel system problems are expensive
* Blue smoke or excessive oil consumption — piston ring wear is likely
* Poor service history — the OM668 is forgiving but not indestructible

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The OM668 is a robust, economical diesel that's well-suited to the Vaneo's practical character. It's generally reliable with a typical lifespan of 240,000–250,000 km. However, injector and EGR issues are common, and the cramped engine bay makes many repairs labour-intensive. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and the 1.7 CDI will reward you with excellent economy and surprising durability. A reconditioned engine from a specialist can be a cost-effective solution if the worst happens.

cta.label: → Compare Vaneo 1.7 CDI engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz Vaneo 1.7 CDI (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Does it start cleanly? Any stalling or cutting out? (injector or fuel system warning)
2. Smoke — Any excessive black or blue smoke? (EGR, turbo or piston ring warning)
3. Service History — including evidence of regular oil changes and major maintenance
4. Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles? (piston ring or turbo warning)
5. Running Quality — Any rough idle or hesitation? (injector or EGR issues)
6. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
7. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (EGR, injectors)
8. Bodywork — Check for rust; the Vaneo is now over 20 years old
